post.content:
**There Are 3 questions to be done in 1 hour.
First question is as Follows:**
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/cf94d1d7-d53d-4b29-ab6e-a479fec90e4c_1659190366.6295393.jpeg)
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/be62c9f9-6c72-4086-b65c-aaf9ba24e82a_1659190378.7979445.jpeg)
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/f5ae356f-0f91-4dfa-bc89-2444ffdad2a7_1659190395.309106.jpeg)
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/2ce00050-6e6a-4a3c-83ec-847b1bc67016_1659190415.815441.jpeg)
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/f6b2422b-424c-4fbc-becc-5ccccded7fbb_1659190424.8016453.jpeg)
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/2f1d71b1-e5b6-4756-afb3-10617b512f5b_1659190434.5495408.jpeg)

**Second Question is: **
The "absolute sum" of an array is defined as the absolute value or the modulus of the sum of its elements.
For example, the array [4,-5,-6] has an absolute sum of 7 while the array [5,-4] has an absolute sum of 1.

**Let us define the "beauty" of an array as the minimum of the absolute sums of all its subarrays.
A "subarray" is defined as a non-empty sequence of consecutive elements of the array.

**An array is "beautiful" if it has a beauty greater than zero.

**For example, the array [-4,5,7] is beautiful with a positive beauty of 1, as its non-empty subarrays [-4], [5], [7], [-4,5], [5,7], [-4,5,7]
have absolute sums of 4, 5, 7, 1, 12 and 8 respectively.**

**On the other hand, the array [-1,1,3] is not beautiful as it has a beauty of 0 because of its subarray [-1,1].**

**Given an array "arr" of length n, your task is to find the number of its subarrays which are beautiful.**
**
**Constraints:
1 ≤ n ≤ 2 \xD7 105**
**−109 ≤ arri ≤ 109**

**Sample Input:**

**arr = [-5,5,7]
Sample Output**:

**4
Explanation:**

**The 4 subarrays [-5], [5], [7] and [5,7] are beautiful with a beauty of 5, 5, 7, 5 respectively.
While the subarrays [-5,5] and [-5,5,7] have a beauty of 0 because of [-5,5] whose absolute sum is zero**

[execution time limit] 1 seconds (cpp)

[input] array.integer arr

Input array

[output] integer64**

**3rd Question is:**
![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/d97908ee-8dd1-463d-85b6-273e91dc8000_1659190549.287342.jpeg)

**Please Share Your Approaches :)**

Q2 a dp problem. calculate the presum up to every index and check if -presum exists. for index i, dp[i] =min(1+dp[i-1], i-the nearest index with -presum)\uFF0C where dp[i] denotes the longest beautiful array ended at i
